summary: The July 10 scout emphasizes QGP azimuthal momentum isotropization, pseudo-gauge ambiguities in local-equilibrium spin polarization, pion nuclear fragmentation functions with A and nu dependence, heavy-quark coalescence with potential effects, and transferable physics AI/ML methods for optimal-transport event-weight resampling, chaotic forecasting, and symbolic regression.
top_takeaway: arXiv:2607.07796 is the strongest heavy-ion paper of the screened set because it directly quantifies relaxation of initial azimuthal momentum anisotropies during QGP thermalization, a live issue for small-system correlation phenomenology.

papers.title: Azimuthal momentum isotropization in the Quark-Gluon Plasma thermalization
papers.authors_short: Sergio Barrera Cabodevila, Xiaojian Du, Carlos A. Salgado, Bin Wu
papers.abstract: Studies relaxation of initial azimuthal momentum anisotropies in QGP thermalization using the Boltzmann Equation in Diffusion Approximation, finding a hierarchy in harmonic relaxation times and pT-dependent harmonic evolution with small-system phenomenology relevance.

papers.title: A symmetry-based resolution of pseudo-gauge ambiguities in local equilibrium
papers.abstract: Reformulates pseudo-gauge and improvement-term ambiguities in local-equilibrium density operators as spurious symmetries with vanishing total charge, proposing a genuine-symmetry prescription relevant to spin polarization in heavy-ion collisions.

papers.title: Analysis of Nuclear Fragmentation Functions for Pions with A and nu Dependence
papers.authors_short: Mengyang Li, Zijian Ye, Jun Gao, Xiaomin Shen, Hongxi Xing, Yuxiang Zhao
papers.abstract: Fits pion nuclear fragmentation functions with nuclear effects parameterized in mass number A, target-rest-frame parton energy nu, and hadron energy fraction z, using SIDIS nuclear data and NLO pp/pA comparisons to ALICE.

papers.title: Heavy quark coalescence probability in the presence of a potential
papers.abstract: Studies how a constituent-quark-model-inspired heavy-quark potential enhances heavy-quark coalescence probability and how moderate in-medium modifications affect the result.

papers.title: Secondary Hadron-Nucleus Collisions of Short-Lived Hadrons in Ultra-Relativistic Fixed-Target Heavy-Ion Interactions
papers.authors_short: Sanatan Digal, P. S. Saumia, Ajit M. Srivastava
papers.abstract: Argues that forward short-lived hadrons from one ultra-relativistic fixed-target heavy-ion encounter may survive to interact with a subsequent nucleus in a solid target, opening unusual secondary hadron-nucleus collision channels.

papers.title: Optimal-Transport-Based Cell Resampling for Negative and Pathological Event Weights
papers.authors_short: Regan Doherty, Lauren Hay, Rishabh Jain, Matt LeBlanc, Julia Marrinan, Camille Mauceri, Jennifer Roloff
papers.abstract: Uses Energy Mover's Distance and related optimal-transport metrics for cell resampling of negative or pathological Monte Carlo event weights, reducing bias in NLO QCD samples relative to prior techniques.

papers.title: A Quantum Reservoir Architecture for Chaotic Forecasting and a Test of Whether Its High Dimension Helps
papers.abstract: Presents a reproducible quantum reservoir architecture for chaotic forecasting and a diagnostic for whether high-dimensional quantum features improve performance beyond capacity effects, tested on a spatiotemporal chain and shallow-water model.

papers.title: DeepPySR - A Symbolic Regression Framework with Dynamic Pruning, Pareto Selection, and Hierarchical Composition for Real-World Scientific Discovery
papers.authors_short: Fuling Chen, Kevin Vinsen, Phillip Melton, Rae-Chi Huang
papers.abstract: Introduces a symbolic-regression framework with dynamic variable pruning, exponential Pareto selection, and hierarchical symbolic composition, evaluated partly on Feynman physics benchmarks.

archive_notes.backfill: The existing homepage lacked the 2026-07-09 card even though metadata and the daily page existed; this run attempted to add both 2026-07-10 and the missing 2026-07-09 entries.
